phases.goal: Identify your stress triggers and establish baseline recovery habits

steps.title: Run a stress audit
steps.description: For 7 days, note the top 3 things that drained you each day and the top 3 that restored you. Most people are surprised to find their biggest stressors are structural (commitments, environment) not situational.

steps.title: Prioritise sleep as your #1 lever
steps.description: Set a consistent bedtime and wake time — even on weekends. Remove screens from your bedroom. Sleep is the single highest-ROI wellbeing intervention. Everything else depends on it.
steps.success_metric: Consistent sleep schedule maintained for 2 weeks

phases.goal: Build daily and weekly recovery rituals that stick

steps.title: Build a morning anchor
steps.description: Create a 15-minute morning routine that starts your day on your terms — before email, before news, before demands. Options: 5 min stretch, 5 min journaling, 5 min breathing. The content matters less than the consistency.
steps.success_metric: Morning routine completed 5+ days per week for 3 weeks

steps.title: Add daily movement
steps.description: 30-minute walks are proven to reduce cortisol, improve mood, and aid sleep. This isn't fitness training — it's a stress management tool. Walking outside in daylight amplifies all three benefits.
steps.success_metric: Daily 30-minute walk completed 5+ days per week

steps.title: Set clear work/rest boundaries
steps.description: Define a hard stop time for work each day. Create a shutdown ritual: write tomorrow's top 3 priorities, close all tabs, step away. This trains your brain to fully disengage, which makes rest actually restorative.
steps.success_metric: Hard stop respected at least 4 days per week for 4 weeks

phases.goal: Embed habits into a long-term system that doesn't require willpower

steps.title: Design your environment for recovery
steps.description: Make the healthy choice the easy choice. Phone charger outside the bedroom, walking shoes by the door, journal on the pillow. Reduce friction for recovery habits and increase friction for stress triggers.
steps.success_metric: Environment redesigned to support 3+ wellbeing habits

steps.title: Weekly review and recalibration
steps.description: Every Sunday, spend 10 minutes reviewing the week: What drained me? What restored me? What do I need to protect or eliminate this coming week? This loop keeps your system self-correcting.
steps.success_metric: Weekly review completed for 2 consecutive weeks
